titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 202
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

p.note: : Mirrored imprint of a removed flyleaf from a missal or breviary.

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil. Inserted leaves of smaller size numbered 63a, 65a, 66a, 148a.

collation.p: Parchment guards, some with visible text.

p.note: Top of page, in brown ink by a contemporary hand: Carthusiensium prope erffordiam est hic liber et continetur in eo, followed by four lines of text (summarizing the contents of the volume) and table of contents. In brown ink by a modern hand: Inköpt till Kgl. Bibliotheket från G. Stephens 23/10 1848. Middle of page, in brown ink by a contemporary hand: Domini Iohannis Staubmels baccalareus theologie et monachus huius domus. In pencil by a modern hand: 242 Blätt. Lower right corner: National Library book label with current signum.

binding.p: Medieval binding. Brown leather over wooden boards. Three double raised bands. Hook and clasp fastenings remaining on and , the strap is lost. On , fragments of a parchment label with partly legible text, beginning Collaciones capitulares, and a second strip of paper with illegible text. On , remnants of a paper label and current signum stamped in gold.

decoNote.p: Tooled in blind in a geometrical pattern of double-lined lozenges.

binding.condition: The leather has been cut, exposing the wood on both and . Board attachments exposed on . The is loose. Holes caused by insects on and .

origin.p:
Germany Salvatorberg charterhouse, Erfurt
before 1464 , based on Lehmann (1928)
. and a note on .

provenance.p:
Following the closure of the charterhouse in , the library was dispersed, see Eifler (2018)
. In the medieval catalogue of Salvatorberg library, the manuscript had the signum O 105, see Lehmann (1928)
.

The volume appears in the sale catalogue of the book collector Friedrich Gottlieb Julius von Bülow (Bibliotheca Büloviana) as lot 437, see Schäfer (1836)
. The volume was purchased at the auction by George Stephens.

acquisition.p: Purchased by the Royal Library from George Stephens in .

msItem.title: On various virtues
msItem.incipit: Secundus panis est oracio primo sciendum primo et principaliter omnes orationes nostras deo porricimus
msItem.explicit: Et potest esse 5tus panis proportionatus (?) io
msItem.note: According to the table of contents on , a Hystoria de parentibus beate virginis should begin on fol. 41 (now lost, the original fol. 49 corresponding to in the modern foliation), but the text on does not seem to correspond to the description in the table of contents.

msItem.title: Exaudi Sunday
msItem.rubric: Dominica exaudi Estote prudentes et vigilate in oracionibus 1a Petri 4
msItem.incipit: Estote prudentes et vigilate in oracionibus (1 Pet. 4:7)
msItem.explicit: Insuper quo usque in hac vita sumus in medio inimicorum poniti sumus vnde supra

msItem.title: First Sunday after Pentecost (Trinity Sunday)
msItem.incipit: dominus proponit exemplum homo non tamquam parabolam
msItem.explicit: Item de quodam abate emnigensis

msItem.title: Third Sunday after Pentecost
msItem.incipit: Erant appropinquantes ad Ihesum publicani et peccatores ut audirent illum (Luke 15:1) publicani sunt qui publica negocia seculi sectantur
msItem.explicit: vnde ego sum panis viuus et cetera
msItem.note: Title taken from the table of contents on . On , in brown ink by a contemporary hand: 8 lines of text, beginning Karissimi humillibamur sub manu potenti and ending with a sentence in German.

msItem.title: Third Sunday after Pentecost
msItem.incipit: Cum turbe irruerunt in ihesum ut audirent illum (Luke 5:1)
msItem.explicit: Sic etiam homines electi restaurantes ruinam
msItem.note: Title taken from the table of contents on .

msItem.title: Unidentified sermon
msItem.incipit: Scilicet audi pastoris diligenciam
msItem.explicit: quod male gessit perpetuumque dampnat
msItem.note: : Upper half of the page blank. The incipit of the sermon is also noted in an addition on .

msItem.title: Easter Sunday
msItem.incipit: Hec dies quam fecit dominus exultemus et letemur in ea (Ps. 117:24) hodie pueri karissimi exultari et letari
msItem.explicit: Reginam celi uel aue maris stella

msItem.title: Tenth Sunday after Pentecost
msItem.rubric: Sermo dominica xa post octauam penthecostes
msItem.incipit: Cum appropinquasset ihesus ierusalem (Matt. 21:1)
msItem.explicit: nos perducare dignetur qui vivit et regnat

msItem.title: On Love
msItem.incipit: Magister sententiarum quia signa dilecconis sunt hec quia amicus amicum libenter audit
msItem.explicit: quod deus diligentibus se preparauit ualeamus pp peruenire

msItem.title: Third Sunday of Easter
msItem.incipit: Moraliter exponendo fidelem animam
msItem.explicit: mulierem grauidam in tergo animam penitentem

msItem.title: Octave of Easter
msItem.incipit: Stetit Iesus in medio discipulorum suorum
msItem.explicit: caret arrogancie et superbie ut dicit gregorius

msItem.title: Nativity of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Fecit rex Salomon thronum de ebore grandem et vestiuit eum auro fuluo nimis 3 Rex 10 (1 Kings 10:18) venerabiles presentes mei in epistula hodierna
msItem.explicit: deus ternus et vnus in secula seculorum benedictus amen

msItem.title: Ascension of Christ
msItem.incipit: Sic veniet quemadmodum vidistis eum (Acts 1:11)
msItem.explicit: super omnia deus in eternum et vltra benedcitus Amen

msItem.title: Ascension of Christ
msItem.incipit: Amice ascende superius et erit tibi gloria (Luke 14:10)
msItem.explicit: in vnitate sancti spiritus cum deo viuit et regnat Amen

msItem.title: Holy angels
msItem.incipit: Angelis suis deus mandauit de te (Ps. 90:11)
msItem.explicit: qui sine fine viuit et regnat Amen

msItem.title: Annunciation of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Descendit ros pariter et manna (Num. 11:9)
msItem.explicit: viuit et regnat deus per secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: Annunciation of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Missus est angelus gabriel a deo (Luke 1:26)
msItem.explicit: o clemens o pia o dulcis maria amen

msItem.title: Annunciation of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: In festiuitate gloriosissime virginis marie
msItem.explicit: in eterna vita beatissimam trinitatem laudare mereamur

msItem.title: Nativity of St John the Baptist
msItem.incipit: Hodierna sollemnitate fratres karissimi
msItem.explicit: Ihesus Christus qui est benedictus in secula etc

msItem.title: Nativity of the Lord
msItem.incipit: Videamus hoc verbum Quale verbum Iohannes testatur dicens
msItem.explicit: viuit et regnat deus per omnia secula seculorum Amen

msItem.title: All Saints
msItem.incipit: Hodie fratres karissimi ecclesia katholica in hac perigrinacione
msItem.explicit: per nomine christi contumeliam pati

msItem.title: Nativity of the Lord
msItem.incipit: Audiuimus hac nocte patres karissimi valde admirabilem musicum
msItem.explicit: sanauit eos et eripiunt eos de intellectionibus eorum

note.p: Additions, possibly the end of sermon, on added in another hand.

msItem.title: Purification of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Inpleti sunt dies purgacionis marie (Luke 2:22)
msItem.explicit: ihesus christus marie filius in secula seculorum benedictus amen

msItem.title: Easter Sunday
msItem.incipit: Que sursum sunt querite (Col. 3:1)
msItem.explicit: querite faciem eius semper (1 Par. 16:11)

msItem.title: Mary Magdalene
msItem.incipit: Maria stabat plorans (John 20:11)
msItem.explicit: in secula seculorum benedictus amen

msItem.title: Nativity of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Orietur stella ex iacob (Num. 24:17)
msItem.explicit: filius in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: Assumption of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Veni in ortum soror mea sponsa (Cant. 5:1)
msItem.explicit: uiuit et regnat in secula seculorm Amen

msItem.title: Mary Magdalene
msItem.incipit: Conuertitur planctum meum in gaudium (Ps. 29:12)
msItem.explicit: uos perducat ihesus christus marie filius in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Mary Magdalene
msItem.incipit: In lectulo meo quesiui quem diligit anima mea (Cant. 3:1)
msItem.explicit: cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Mary Magdalene
msItem.incipit: Ora pro nobis quoniam mulier sancta es (Judith 8:29)
msItem.explicit: viuit et regnat deus in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Ascension of Christ
msItem.incipit: Assumptus est Ihesus Christus
msItem.explicit: cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: Assumption of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: En introduxit uirum ebreum (Gen. 39:15)
msItem.explicit: cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Nativity of the Lord
msItem.incipit: Misit deus filium suum factum ex muliere (Gal. 4:4)
msItem.explicit: filius tuus hodie puer natus in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Hugh of Lincoln
msItem.rubric: Sermo in festo hugonis episcopi lynconiensis
msItem.incipit: Iustus iusticiam faciat (1 John 3:7)
msItem.explicit: ihesus christus marie filius in eternitate benedictus Amen

msItem.title: Hugh of Lincoln
msItem.rubric: Item sermo in festo hugonis episcopi lyncolniensis
msItem.incipit: Iustum deduxit dominus per vias rectas (Wisdom 10:10)
msItem.explicit: ihesus christus marie filius in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: All Saints
msItem.incipit: Laulate dominus in sanctis eius (Ps. 150:1)
msItem.explicit: ihesus christus cum patre et spiritu in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: All Saints
msItem.incipit: Sancti per fidem vicerunt regna (Heb. 11:33)
msItem.explicit: ihesus christus cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: Angels
msItem.incipit: Angeli eorum vident faciem patris mei (Matt. 18:10)
msItem.explicit: cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus Amen

msItem.title: All Saints
msItem.incipit: Estis ciues sanctorum et domestici dei (Eph. 2:19)
msItem.explicit: cum patre et spiritu sancto in secula seculorum benedictus

msItem.title: Hugh of Lincoln
msItem.rubric: Sermo in festo hugonis episcopi lyncolniensis
msItem.incipit: Ecce sacerdos magnus qui in diebus suis placuit Deo
msItem.explicit: desiderio eorum anhelabat informari exemplo

msItem.title: John the Evangelist
msItem.incipit: Dilectus deo et hominibus (Eccl. 45:1)
msItem.explicit: cum omnibus sanctis regnat sine fine amen

msItem.title: Easter Monday
msItem.incipit: Mane nobiscum domine quoniam aduesperascit (Luke 24:29)
msItem.explicit: per infinita secula seculorum Amen
msItem.note: The text is followed by a six-line addendum in the same hand.

msItem.title: Easter Tuesday
msItem.incipit: Stetit ihesus in medio discipulorum (Luke 24:36)

msItem.title: All Saints
msItem.incipit: Tamquam aurum in fornace probauit illos (Wisdom 3:6)
msItem.explicit: benedictus in secula seculorum Amen

msItem.title: Visitations
msItem.incipit: Descendi in ortum meum (Cant. 6:10)
msItem.explicit: et absque velaminem propalantem etcetera

msItem.title: Election of a prior
msItem.incipit: Videte quomodo caute ambuletis (Eph. 5:15)
msItem.explicit: qui in electis suis mittit radices et cetera

msItem.title: Nativity of the Lord
msItem.incipit: Paruulus natus est nobis et filius datus est nobis (Isaiah 9:6)
msItem.explicit: uel in celo propter sue ruine

msItem.title: Prelates
msItem.incipit: Amicus noster dormit sed vado sed vado ut a sompno excitem eum (Joh. 11:11)
msItem.explicit: et perfectum erit gaudium meum quod vobis concedat et cetera

msItem.title: Annunciation of the Virgin
msItem.incipit: Hec dies boni nuncii est (2 Kings 7:9)
msItem.explicit: ab operibus mortuis ad uenindum (Heb. 9:14)

msItem.title: Assumption of the Virgin (incomplete)
msItem.incipit: Regina saba ingressa est in ierusalem (3 Kings 10:1)
msItem.explicit: sola videlicet anima breuiter dicendum est
msItem.note: Title from a similar text in Bodelian MS Hamilton 44, with Erfurt provenance. : Blank page.

msItem.title: Easter Sunday
msItem.incipit: Surrexit Mt ultimo
msItem.explicit: qui cum patre et spiritu sancto sine fine viuit et regnat Amen

msItem.title: Sts Peter and Paul
msItem.incipit: Quia sicut ait gregorius 6 moralium Quam talibus doctrina mens polleat
msItem.explicit: dignetur dominus ihesus christus amen

msItem.title: General Chapter
msItem.incipit: Sobrii estote et vigilate (1 Pet. 5:8)
msItem.explicit: Quod nobis omnibus concedat qui sine fine viuit et regnat amen

p.note: Watermarks only partly visible, for example bull's heads.

supportDesc.condition: Several leaves missing at the beginning and end of the volume. Holes caused by insects especially at the beginning and end of the volume.

layout.note: Occasional guidelines in ink, for example on .

handDesc.p: Several 15th century hands. The cursive hands are very similar and the description is tentative.
handDesc.handNote: Gothic cursive; this is probably the hand of Johannes Staubmel.

p.note:
Marginal annotations throghout the volume. A contemporary hand has added rubrics to some sermons in light brown ink: : De resureccione domini : Notabilia de dileccione
Dominica 3a post resureccionem : Octaua post resurrectionem domini : Collacio de beata virgine : In ascensione domini collacio : De ascensione domini : In die sanctorum angelorum : In annunciacione beate virginis collacio : In annunciacione : In annunciacione beate virginis : Honora patrem tuum Exodi xx:o : In natiuitate domini collacio : In natiuitate domini collacio breuis : De maria magdalena : In nativitate marie : Marie magdelene : In electione prioris : Collacio in resurreccione domini : Sermo siue collacio de sanctis petro et paulo : Collacio de Concilio
In brown ink by a contemporary hand: 8 lines of text, beginning Karissimi humillibamur sub manu potenti and ending with a sentence in German.
Bottom of page, in brown ink by a contemporary hand (possibly the same as the main text): 9 lines of text, beginning Nota quod ihesus stetit in medio discipulorum.
17-line addition at the bottom of the page, in the same hand as the main scribe.
Upper left corner, in brown ink by a contemporary hand: annotation partly lost due to trimming of the page.
In brown ink by a contemporary hand: marginal annotations and the end of the sermon.

Top of page, in light brown ink by a late medieval hand: Secuntur 18 collaciones capitulares domini iohannes staubmels baccalarii formati sacre scripture professi domus erffordiensis. Added to the right: Prima.

Lower half of the page, in brown ink by a late medieval hand: prescripte collactiones collecte sunt per dominum iohannem staubmel sacre theologie baccalarium formatum et huius domus sancti saluatoris prope erffordiam monachum professum qui obiit anno mccclxiiii in vigilia sancte trinitiatis, followed by a table of contents numbered 1-12
Top of page, in brown ink by a contemporary hand: three lines of text, beginning Sermo de festo Iohanne ewangeliste.
In brown ink by a contemporary hand: addition to a sermon.
In dark brown ink by a contemporary hand: 12 lines of text. 2 lines of text at the bottom of the page.

decoNote.p: Bookmarks in brown leather on ; ; ; ; ; in parchment on ; ; ; ; ; ; ; .
: Penwork initial A with internal reserved shapes, in brown ink over 3 lines, 7 lines extensions included.
